item.title: Woo!
item.dc:creator: fulcrims

item.title: N°n-p°litical p°st
item.description: That night, Tuesday night ... That night so many of us started deleting our social media accounts. That night I had to talk down three. THREE. Three women from un∆living themselves. Because they are trans. Like me. We know what's coming. We know what they have said they want to do. We know what they are legally able to do. Most frighteningly we know our history. I'm going to stay and survive. I'm going to fight for others. But I'm NOT OK EMOTIONALLY NOT OK Having to talk friends down, knowing that not everyone will make it through the coming time. Yeah. Yeah... #Ineedafuckinghug
item.dc:creator: ThatOtherGirlYouKnow

item.title: Shinobi Sunday: Naruto
item.description: Model: Simply Arabella Did a post about an hour ago with several cosplays, and a few...not really cosplays. That was yeeted, I guess it was pushing it. So here's something simple, cosplayer and reference image. Probably the most detailed one, I'm so impressed by people's creativity.

item.title: This post is devastating! Goodest boy Bruce was abandoned by his family and tied to a tree near Humane Society of Broward! Please share this video everywhere and let's find Bruce a forever home so he can have a forever family for Thanksgiving!
item.description:
If you know anyone looking for a devoted and loving good boy, please send them to Humane Society of Broward.
https://humanebroward.com/pet/?id=A689499&type=Dog&pg=1&fbclid=PAY2xjawGctypleHRuA2FlbQIxMAABpoOhyHtqp2p2Dbkh_JQB2uP51wORGqr2EHbLyS2LG0gWzExVwW_rj7F36w_aem_o7Xta_N5Uldi6EYGOIV41g

item.title: Viking weekend!
item.description:
My fit for Viking weekend at Ren faire! The sun was in my eyes and I was extra squinty so have a skull face
Here's my face makeup Is it socially acceptable to wear Viking makeup everyday? Asking for myself. Also I apparently like peace signs. That is all.

item.title: Lost my Dad on Thursday
item.description: Last messages between us. I am so grateful the last thing I told my Dad was that we love him. Please tell your loved ones. Give them a hug. If you think of something nice about someone, say it to them. Love you guys. Good night.
item.dc:creator: brotrustme

item.title: The Veiled Lady
item.description: "The Veiled Lady" Bust sculpted in 1869 by the Italian artist Giovanni Battista Lombardi. Famous for his sculptures of romantic and sentimental appeal, Lombardi applied a technique to marble that gives us the impression of a thin veil covering the lady's saddened face. If we look hastily, we have the impression that the knot below the chin was made with real fabric. The diaphanous gauze shows his melancholy expression, revealing his naked bust underneath. Even the veil lace was made by the sculptor, further increasing the realism of this masterpiece. https://collections.artsmia.org/art/12092/veiled-lady-raffaelo-monti
item.dc:creator: TheRainbowegoSweet007

item.title: NEVER AGAIN
item.description: Take a couple minutes today to teach tolerance. A small step by each person adds up to huge systemic changes.

item.title: Did a fun floral hairstyle for a photoshoot last weekend!!
item.description:
My friend Sarah reached out about doing this photoshoot with a cascading floral hairstyle. I had a really fun time helping bring her creative idea to life! Her handle is @soko.photos on instagram, I definitely recommend checking out her work!! https://www.instagram.com/soko.photos
A few of my favorite shots
Have a lovely weekend, friends!

item.title: Thought more people should see this
item.description: Sauce: https://youtu.be/iX3vMJOADlE?si=WgeiMZiKABXqwd6K Youtuber: Led By Donkeys Video Title: TESLA = TRUMP Edit. After reading some of the comments, I just wanted to say and remind everyone that it's important to keep these atrocities in the limelight. If we stop talking about them and give into the harmful rhetoric (by doing nothing), we tip the scales in favor of those that want to surpress human rights. Please dont bury your head in the sand. The least we can do is stay united and keep spreading the word to reach more people. Also watch the video. I only took snippets and it covers events up until the election.
item.dc:creator: PineySweets

item.title: I explored various abandoned places on the Island of Tenerife (Canaries), Spain (OC)
item.description:
This is the building shell of an unfinished hotel in Añaza. I discovered it while on vacation on the Canaries in early 2023. If you like, join me on a little urbex tour across Tenerife and see what an island the Atlantic has to offer in terms of abandoned places!
We start with an exclusive little object, that is almost impossible to notice from the landside. If you don't know it's there, there are very few signs, such as this small metal frame in the background if you wander along an overgrown trail almost until the end.
Knowing the right spots though, you can catch a nice look at "El Esqueleto". The abandoned construction site of an unfinished hotel now just sits there on a prime location at the coast. Even more interesting: it dates back to 1972, meaning it has been in this state for over 50 years!
If you zoom in, you can still see piles of material being neatly set up as if the construction would continue tomorrow. Also: imagine booking a room here and then getting one with "Garden View" =)
Another interesting little object is the "Casa Hamilton" on the north coast. Its original purpose was housing a steam-engine, which powered a pump, providing water for agricultural purposes.
The surroundings are heavily secured and entering despite that it is expressedly discouraged due to a variety of dangers. Coming to the same assessment and decide against entering anyway.
Although it's certainly great for a couple of nice pictures, it is mostly empty and already partially collapsed, so there probably won't be much to discover anyway. Admiring it from afar is the better option here =).
There is a nice view spot, which you can use to learn about the history as well. The building dates back to 1903 and considering that it hasn't been maintained in decades, it's still holding up nicely against the waves! Interestingly, it's the only site in our collection that actually fulfilled its intended purpose.
Speaking of which, I present Abades! The Franco regime had this built in the early 1940s, when Leprosy was spreading severely, and infected people were segregated into colonies like these. Fortunately, new cures became available before the colony was finished. So, it's basically just sitting there for over 80 years now.
The most prominent building and thus important point of orientation is the church. It's as unfinished and in a raw, concrete-y state as everything else, but the purpose is clear.
The light setting in here in the late afternoon was awesome. I can totally see how they would have had powerful services here.
Most other buildings were in about that state. Raw walls, tiles in some places, everything in comparatively decent shape.
This would have been the school, which was the biggest building by far. They certainly planned on accommodating children here for longer periods of time.
Yes, these are bullet holes. From what I hear, the site was used by Spanish military and police units to practice urban tactical combat scenarios.
I'm not sure if this damage was part of that or just the usual vandalism which sadly occurs over time. What this room was intended to be used for is your guess. It might have been a kiosk or kitchen area.
Washing rooms perhaps? I would assume that hygiene would have been immensely important in an isolation setting.
Which I also probably why most of the floors and some of the walls are tiled (or at least used to be).
This would probably have been the gymnasium, which was quite decent in size. All in all, a weird place to explore. Such a gloomy background in such a pretty location directly at the coast. Thank God they never actually needed to use it.
Circling back to the cover picture: the Añaza hotel. This 23-story monstrosity is the result of a bankrupt venture in the 70s, which wanted to operate a hotel and sell some apartments directly at the coast. Like the Esqueleto, it sits here for decades and decades in its perpetually unfinished state, defacing the coastline.
Compared to the Esqueleto it is easily accesible though. There is a fence with a gate in it, but the gate is right open, soooo ... let's head inside.
A closer look reveals that the construction indeed doesn't seem to have gone further than creating the foundation, frame and some of the shell.
Coming closer the building gets really tall. The Y-shaped architecture is interesting, clearly aiming for having as many ocean-view rooms as possible.
Peeking inside, it turns out, this thing is REALLY unfinished. Exploring it is EXTREMELY dangerous, and I strongly advise against it. I am not kidding; several people have died here and for good reason.
One wrong step and you could fall like 40 m down in this elevator shaft.
Or maybe this dark sewage pipe to never be heard from again. The main staircase also is pretty narrow and not secured at all with just one giant whole in the middle, making for a nice 20-story fall. No wonder kids in the area make going up there a dare.
If you actually do make it to some of the rooms though, you could imagine that the view from some of them must have been really very pretty.
The rest of the structure mostly consists of dark hallways, some also with holes in the bottom, and rubble, increasing the chance of tripping even more. Still in this case, you should NOT follow the light, at least not too far ...
... or you'll end up straight in the Atlantic Ocean. The place is supposed to be demolished in the coming years and although I'm all for preserving a good abandoned place, in this case this sure seems like the better option. +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++ I explored a variety of abandoned places on the Spanish island of Tenerife. All pictures were taken with a Canon 80D and a Samsung S22+ and are original content. For further information on the site, please visit the following sources: - https://tiempodecanarias.com/noticia/planeta/borrador-reportaje-%60el-esqueleto%C2%B4 - https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Elevador_de_Aguas_de_Gordejuela - https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Abades,_Tenerife - https://whalenationblog.wordpress.com/2021/01/07/dark-tourism-in-tenerife-visiting-abades-abandoned-leper-colony/ - https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hotel_A%C3%B1aza - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q2KeQ9I5VG0 - https://www.express.co.uk/news/world/1892900/Tenerife-abandoned-hotel-thrill-seekers-died DISCLAIMER: Exploring sites like this one might be trespassing and thus is most likely illegal if done unauthorizedly. Also, it might be rather dangerous, since there is damage and decay. I do not encourage anyone to explore such a place ... if you should decide to do it anyway, follow the basic rules of urban exploration: 1. Do not go alone 2. Watch your steps closely 3. Leave everything the way you find it 4. If something seems unstable or too dangerous, take another route or simply don't go there at all 5. Wear adequate clothing, stable shoes and bring sensible equipment (such as a flashlight and a cell phone) Life and health are more relevant than curiosity ... but it's also important to have fun ;) If you're interested in seeing more morbid, decaying goodness, please consider checking out my other posts: - Semi-Abandoned Town of Bombay Beach, Salton Sea, California (OC): https://imgur.com/gallery/i-explored-semi-abandoned-town-of-bombay-beach-salton-sea-ca-sandy-shores-from-gta-v-oc-byp2wws - Abandoned Lake Dolores Waterpark in the Mojave Desert, California (OC): https://imgur.com/gallery/7llNW3M - Abandoned George Air Force Base in Victorville, California (OC): https://imgur.com/gallery/8H0uAao - Abandoned HQ bunker of the Ministery of the Interior of the GDR: https://imgur.com/gallery/6wIRqnC - Abandoned "Sophienheilstätte" sanatorium in Thuringia, Germany: https://imgur.com/gallery/aQyPZkQ - Abandoned Hotel "Schwarzeck" in Thuringia, Germany: https://imgur.com/gallery/p9K55dg - Abandoned Luxury Hotel Fürstenhof in Eisenach, Germany: https://imgur.com/gallery/q2qdGpd - Abandoned Petrova Gora Spomenik in Croatia: https://imgur.com/gallery/QEXrm - Abandoned luxury hotel "Belvedere" near Dubrovnik: https://imgur.com/gallery/SYbeQ - Abandoned hotel "Jadran" in Jelsa on the island Hvar: https://imgur.com/gallery/6peUy
item.dc:creator: netcrack2k
